content.problemStatement:
Amr has given you an array AAA of size NNN and an integer XXX.

Let F(L,R)F(L, R)F(L,R) denote the number of pairs (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) such that:


L‚Č§i<j‚Č§RL \le i \lt j \le RL‚Č§i<j‚Č§R;
Ai+AjA_i + A_jAi‚Äč+Aj‚Äč has **exactly** XXX digits.
Your task is to calculate the value ‚ąĎL=1N‚ąĎR=L+1NF(L,R)\displaystyle{\sum_{L=1}^N \sum_{R=L+1}^N F(L, R)}L=1‚ąĎN‚ÄčR=L+1‚ąĎN‚ÄčF(L,R).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX ‚ÄĒ the size of the array and the desired number of digits in the sum, respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, representing array elements.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the value ‚ąĎL=1N‚ąĎR=L+1NF(L,R)\displaystyle{\sum_{L=1}^N \sum_{R=L+1}^N F(L, R)}L=1‚ąĎN‚ÄčR=L+1‚ąĎN‚ÄčF(L,R).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2√ó1052 \times 10^52√ó105.

F(1,3)=3F(1, 3) = 3F(1,3)=3: Pairs (1,2),(2,3),(1, 2), (2, 3),(1,2),(2,3), and (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) resulting in sum 10,19,10, 19,10,19, and 111111 respectively, each with 222 digits.
F(2,3)=1F(2, 3) = 1F(2,3)=1: The only pair is (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) resulting in A2+A3=19A_2+A_3=19A2‚Äč+A3‚Äč=19 with 222 digits.

Test case 222: There are 222 valid pairs:

F(1,2)=1F(1, 2) = 1F(1,2)=1: The only pair is (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) resulting in A1+A2=31A_1+A_2=31A1‚Äč+A2‚Äč=31 with 222 digits.
F(1,3)=1F(1, 3) = 1F(1,3)=1: The only pair is (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) resulting in A1+A2=31A_1+A_2=31A1‚Äč+A2‚Äč=31 with 222 digits.
F(2,3)=0F(2, 3) = 0F(2,3)=0
